data.title: Bitcoin market fundamentals ‘couldn't be better,’ says Strategy CEO
data.description:
Bitcoin’s market fundamentals have stayed strong in 2025, despite the asset’s price and sentiment declining toward the end of the year, says Strategy CEO Phong Le.

“The fundamentals of the market this year for Bitcoin couldn’t be better,” Le told the “Coin Stories” podcast on Tuesday, emphasizing that he doesn’t care too much about its short-term performance. 

Bitcoin (BTC) reached an all-time high of $125,100 on Oct. 5, but has since declined nearly 30%, trading at $87,687 at the time of publication, according to CoinMarketCap. Meanwhile, the Crypto Fear & Greed Index, which measures overall market sentiment, has shown “Extreme Fear” since Dec. 12.

Le acknowledged that Bitcoin’s price “does what it does” and isn’t always easy to explain. 

“When you’re an investor, you think about the long term of the asset class,” he said. 

Bitcoiners should be “fairly methodical” about short-term price

Le stressed that short-term price action is often unpredictable and Bitcoiners should be “fairly methodical and mathematical about it.” 

“Which is why we focus on things like mNAV, why we built out the Bitcoin treasury and why we built out the US dollar treasury,” he said.

Alongside Bitcoin’s price decline, Strategy’s (MSTR) mNAV, the company’s market value compared to the value of its Bitcoin holdings, has fallen below 1, trading at 0.93, according to Saylor Tracker. The company holds 671,268 Bitcoin worth around $58.63 billion.

Looking at the long-term fundamentals, he pointed to the US government being “fully supportive of Bitcoin like it’s never been before.” 

TradFi is trying to “figure out” how to catch up

Le said that he and Strategy executive chairman, Michael Saylor, have been meeting with traditional banks across the US and UAE, where institutions are trying to figure out how to catch up.

“If you think about what’s happening with traditional powers of the world. The US government, the US banking system, they are all getting on board with Bitcoin,” Le said.

“That’s extremely bullish for this year and 2026,” Le added. While US President Trump signed the executive order officially establishing the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and US Digital Asset Stockpile in March, a formalized strategic plan has not been confirmed yet. 

Some analysts had forecasted it coming to fruition this year.

Galaxy Digital’s head of firmwide research, Alex Thorn, said in September that “there’s a strong chance the US government will announce this year that it has formed the strategic Bitcoin reserve.”

data.title: Yen Mostly Weakens Amid Risk-on Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: The yen weakens against most other G-10 and Asian currencies in the early session amid risk-on sentiment spurred by gains in Japan's stock market. Japanese economic data released earlier also showed Tokyo consumer prices, excluding fresh food, in December rose a slower-than-expected 2.3% on year. The data could soften the case for the BOJ to raise rates quickly and weigh on the yen. USD/JPY rises 0.3% to 156.19, AUD/JPY adds 0.4% to 104.80, and EUR/JPY is 0.4% higher at 184.15, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Political stability at stake as Malaysia's Najib awaits verdict in biggest 1MDB trial
data.description:
Key points:
  • Najib faces more years in jail, hefty fines if convicted
  • Ruling could strain ties in PM Anwar's government
  • Court to decide 4 more graft charges, 21 counts of money laundering

By Rozanna Latiff

Jailed former Malaysian Prime Minister Najib Razak will hear a verdict on Friday in the biggest trial he faces over the multibillion-dollar 1MDB scandal, a ruling that could risk deepening tensions within the administration of current premier Anwar Ibrahim.

Investigators have said about $4.5 billion was allegedly stolen from 1Malaysia Development Berhad, a state fund co-founded by Najib in 2009, and that more than $1 billion allegedly made its way into his personal bank accounts.

Najib, 72, last year apologised for mishandling the scandal while in office but he has consistently denied wrongdoing, saying he was misled by 1MDB officials and a fugitive financier, Jho Low, on the source of the funds.

In 2020, Najib was convicted of graft and money laundering for illegally receiving funds from a 1MDB unit and began a 12-year prison sentence two years later after losing all his appeals. That sentence was later halved by a pardons board chaired by Malaysia's king, with Najib due for release in 2028.

A Malaysian high court will decide on Friday whether to convict Najib of four more charges of corruption and 21 counts of money laundering involving the illegal transfer of about 2.2 billion ringgit ($539 million) from 1MDB.

If found guilty, he could face maximum jail terms of between 15 and 20 years on each charge, as well as a fine of up to five times the value of the alleged misappropriations.

The implementation of the penalties, however, could be stayed pending further appeals.

VERDICTS TEST GOVERNMENT STABILITY

The decision will be closely watched after another court this week dismissed a bid by Najib to serve the remainder of his sentence under house arrest.

That ruling reignited tensions in Anwar's ruling administration, which includes Najib's party, the United Malays National Organisation.

UMNO campaigned against Anwar in a 2022 election but joined his coalition to form a government after the poll ended in a hung parliament.

Several UMNO leaders expressed disappointment with the decision to deny Najib house arrest, saying it risked diluting the powers of Malaysia's rulers, while others were angered by social media posts by some members of Anwar's coalition celebrating the ruling.

Anwar this week called for all parties to handle news of the verdict with patience and wisdom, adding that it was "inappropriate to muddy the atmosphere or add tension" even if there were those who chose not to sympathize with Najib and his family.

A guilty verdict for Najib on Friday could strain ties further, with some UMNO leaders already calling for the party to review its pact with Anwar or withdraw from the government altogether. An acquittal, however, may weaken Anwar, who has been under pressure to uphold his credentials as an anti-graft campaigner.

Anwar has been accused by critics of betraying progressive voters and allies after prosecutors dropped some corruption charges against Najib and other UMNO figures. The premier has repeatedly said he does not interfere in court cases.

data.title: Japan's Industrial Production Falls But Rebound Expected
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--Japan's industrial output retreated in November amid the persistent drag of U.S. tariffs on the manufacturing sector and mounting concerns over a global economic slowdown.

Industrial production fell 2.6%, reversing from October's 1.5% rise, according to government data released on Friday. The drop was steeper than the 1.8% decline expected in a poll of economists by data provider Quick.

Many Japanese exports to the U.S. still incur 15% tariffs after Tokyo and Washington reached a trade deal earlier this year. The focus remains on whether these costs will erode Japanese firms' investment plans.

However, Friday's data showed that companies expect output to recover soon, forecasting a 1.3% increase for December and an 8.0% jump in January.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: JGBs Edge Higher as Investors Digest Tokyo Inflation Data — Market Talk
data.description: JGBs edge higher in price terms as investors digest Tokyo inflation data released earlier. On the one hand, the December data showed consumer prices in Tokyo, excluding fresh food, rose 2.3% on year, a slower pace than the 2.5% increase expected in a poll of economists by data provider Quick. On the other hand, the reading stayed above the BOJ's 2% target, keeping the central bank on track for further monetary tightening. Concerns over the weak yen and inflation as well as expectations of continued BOJ rate increases are deeply rooted, Mitsubishi UFJ Morgan Stanley Securities' fixed-income strategists say in commentary.The 10-year JGB yield is down 1 bp at 2.035%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Nikkei Gains 0.6%, Led by Tech, Videogame Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks are higher as the yen weakens following softer-than-expected Tokyo inflation data. Tech and videogame stocks are leading gains. SoftBank Group is up 1.5% and Nintendo is 1.6% higher. USD/JPY is at 156.22, up from 155.81 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching any comments on the yen's recent depreciation from Japanese government officials as well as any progress in PM Sanae Takaichi's economic plans. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.6% at 50700.49. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Egypt Cuts Rate 100 Bps to 20%
data.description:
The Central Bank of Egypt lowered key interest rates by 100 bps on Thursday, 25 December 2025, signalling growing confidence that inflationary pressures are easing after a prolonged period of tight monetary policy.

The decision brought the interest rate to its lowest level since January 2024.

The Monetary Policy Committee also slashed the overnight deposit rate to 20.0%, the overnight lending rate to 21.0%, and the main operation rate to 20.50%.

The discount rate was also cut to 20.50%.

data.title: Key facts: XRP Stochastic RSI Hits 0.00; Novogratz Backs Investment; Reserve Inclusion Boosts Wealth
data.description:
• XRP's Stochastic RSI has dropped to 0.00 on a 3-week chart, a rare sign that may indicate a price rally, suggesting selling pressure is waning and long-term holders are buying.1
  • Galaxy CEO Mike Novogratz has changed his view on XRP, moving from doubt to investment, and commended the robust community supporting the asset amid the SEC lawsuit against Ripple.2
  • Joshua Dalton of Triblu noted that XRP holders could gain wealth if XRP is included in a US crypto reserve, citing its ties to a US company as a potential advantage over Bitcoin.3

data.title: Key facts: User loses $50M in Tether attack; 53% of USDT frozen on Tron
data.description:
• A user lost almost $50 million in Tether (USDT) from an address poisoning attack, where funds were sent to a fake address mimicking a real one, highlighting blockchain vulnerabilities.1
  • Over 53% of frozen Tether (USDT) is on the Tron network, known for fast, low-cost transfers. Tether can burn and reissue tokens to address scams.2

data.title: Key facts: Ethereum ETFs Surge; Price Hits Record $4,953.73; ePBS Enhances Security
data.description:
• By late 2025, demand for Ethereum ETFs surged, with 11 available, causing a supply squeeze that drove Ethereum's price to a record high of $4,953.73.1
  • Ethereum continues to lead in the financial ecosystem with a greater total value locked (TVL) and stablecoin usage, despite competition from Solana, which is noted for its speed and adaptability.2
  • Ethereum's integration of Enshrined Proposer Builder Separation (ePBS) boosts security and decentralization, enabling more time for generating and validating zero-knowledge (ZK) proofs.3

data.title: Key facts: Cardano Price Drop Triggers $167K Liquidations; $50M Loss Reported; Hoskinson Denies ADA Sale Rumors
data.description:
• Cardano (ADAUSD) saw a significant price drop, leading to $167,850 in long position liquidations within an hour, creating a 66,530% liquidation imbalance against just $243 in shorts.1
  • Charles Hoskinson highlighted a $50 million loss from an address poisoning attack, noting that Cardano's UTXO model offers better protection against such scams than Ethereum's account-based model.2
  • Charles Hoskinson, Cardano's founder, denied rumors of selling ADA tokens at a high price in 2021, claiming he never sold any ADA and calling the allegations false.3

data.title: Key facts: Bitcoin options worth $23.7B expire; ETFs face outflows
data.description:
• On December 26, Bitcoin options worth $23.7 billion will expire, likely leading to price stability as institutions adjust their positions to optimize profits.1
  • Bitcoin shows little correlation with the Nasdaq and a negative one with gold, indicating a shift towards being viewed as a high-risk asset, leading to stagnant capital inflows.23
  • Major Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) have experienced continuous outflows since November, reflecting a trend of institutional disengagement from Bitcoin (BTCUSD).4
